verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Main blocker: Surfline consistency is lully.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY there’s probably no reliable window to chase. Rey Cortiço at lunch is the best of what’s available, but the wind looks like the main problem and Surfline consistency is really poor, so it could end up too small and messy. I’d check Surfline and MEO Beachcam before leaving, and if they don’t look noticeably better, I’d save the drive.

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0-0.3 m, 20 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot; exposed north spot probably too small.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
exposed north spot probably too small

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 19 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
Cerro may feel too soft without more push

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 10 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; Meio da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size; Meio da Baia may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (18:00 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a small window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 19 kJ, 15 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 14s+ at mid tide still tends to close out on exposed beaches.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot; 14s+ at mid tide still tends to close out on exposed beaches; exposed north spot probably too small.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide makes this one less convincing.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
14s+ at mid tide still tends to close out on exposed beaches
exposed north spot probably too small
